mentions.note: Cramer likes it—very good unique property—but at 29x earnings, too expensive to buy aggressively. Buy slowly over time, not all at once.
Mentioned alongside DoorDash and Uber as software stocks being rotated out in favor of semiconductor hardware plays.
7 quarters >60% revenue growth; 90% gross margins; $1M CapEx; essential AI training data moat; ad revenue up 74%; terrific story down 36% YTD.
Rallied from $141 to $162 post-Nectar Social deal. Very valuable company; Cramer would snap it up if could for full corpus acquisition ($31B). Continue to own; undervalued earlier.
$31B business very valuable; Cramer would snap it up if bigger; up $42 in recent weeks but not too far, too fast; own it; training data valuable for AI.
Social software platform down 40%; stock suffering despite good underlying business (active communities, engagement); caught in software sector rotation.

Litigation overhang causing pullback; Cramer sees opportunity and will buy gingerly (stock trades in 12-point increments). Lawsuits likely to be overturned; don't let J&J/Berkshire repeat (sold too early) happen again.
Down 30% post-earnings and great quarter/buyback, trading at 27x earnings on oversold market bounce; valuable ad-supported platform charging too little for advertising.
Down 50% from all-time highs; CEO Steve Huffman executing well; solid product with strong user engagement; wild trader but fundamentals solid.
Great quarter, guidance, $1B buyback initiated, but hammered on growth concerns. In Cramer's 'How to Make Money in Any Market' top-5 worthy at current valuation; worth more than current price.

Seems to move in 20 point increments every time anybody says anything; bet it's up this time; reports Thursday.
